texto: The love of Christ for me will get last say. He is merciful to me for his name’s sake, for the sake of his own goodness, for the sake of his steadfast love and compassion (Psalm 25). When he thinks about me, he remembers what he is like, and that is my exceeding joy. My indestructible hope is that he has turned his face towards me, and he will never turn away.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a David A. Powlison: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “The love of Christ for me will get last say. He is merciful to me for his name’s sake, for the sa…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, god, esperanca pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de esperanca e theology.

frase_original: The love of Christ for me will get last say. He is merciful to me for his name’s sake, for the sake of his own goodness, for the sake of his steadfast love and compassion (Psalm 25). When he thinks about me, he remembers what he is like, and that is my exceeding joy. My indestructible hope is that he has turned his face towards me, and he will never turn away.
